title: Syzygium tephrodes
intro: Syzygium tephrodes, commonly known as the brown leaved water apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The health benefits of Syzygium tephrodes include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in managing various health conditions such as infections and inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Syzygium tephrodes is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to manage fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Syzygium tephrodes is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species in the family. Syzygium tephrodes is further classified as a species within the genus Syzygium, which includes approximately 1,300 species of flowering plants. The genus Syzygium is a member of the subfamily Myrtoideae, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their showy flowers and aromatic leaves. Overall, the classification of Syzygium tephrodes reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Myrtaceae.
